Call 2025 - Round 3
Mobility of young people (KA152-YOU)
Form ID: KA152-YOU-BBD01623 Deadline (Brussels Time): 01 Oct 2025 12:00:00
Project Summary
Please provide short answers to the following questions to summarise the information you have provided in the rest of the
application form.
Please use full sentences and clear language. In case your project is accepted, the summary you provided will be made
public by the European Commission and the National Agencies.
What do you want to achieve by implementing the project? What are the objectives of your project? Please specify from the
perspective of youth work practice.
Our project aims to support youth engagement in advocacy and strengthen their understanding and identification with the
European Union identity. Through non-formal and informal educational methods, we seek to empower young people to
become active, informed, and influential members of their communities. The specific objectives we intend to achieve:
First, we aim to enhance the advocacy skills and civic engagement of young participants, enabling them to effectively voice
their opinions and influence policy decisions at local, national, and European levels. Advocacy is crucial for youth to actively
participate in democratic processes and ensure their voices are heard. By developing these skills, we empower young
people to become catalysts for change in their communities and beyond. To achieve this, we will conduct interactive
workshops on advocacy techniques, public speaking, and lobbying. Participants will engage in role-playing and simulation
exercises to practice and refine their advocacy strategies. They will also design and implement advocacy campaigns on
issues relevant to them, providing hands-on experience in planning and execution. Furthermore, we will connect participants
with experienced advocates and policymakers through mentorship programs, offering guidance and support throughout
their advocacy journeys.
Second, we aim to strengthen young participants’ understanding of the European Union’s history, values, and institutions,
fostering a strong sense of EU identity and belonging. A robust EU identity encourages unity, cooperation, and a shared
sense of purpose among member states. It helps young people appreciate the benefits of EU membership and the
importance of collective action. We will use non-formal educational methods, such as interactive lectures, group
discussions, and multimedia presentations, to teach participants about the EU’s history, values, and functioning.
Additionally, we will organize intercultural nights where participants from different EU countries can share their cultures,
traditions, and perspectives, promoting mutual understanding and respect. Participants will also engage in EU simulation
games, such as model European Parliament sessions, to gain practical insights into how the EU operates. Furthermore, the
activity where we will host a stakeholder would also be necessary for participants to understand better the EU structure and
dynamics.
Third, we aim to promote intercultural collaboration and understanding among young participants from diverse
backgrounds, enhancing social cohesion and reducing prejudices. Intercultural collaboration is vital in a multicultural EU as
it helps young people appreciate diversity, build cross-cultural friendships, and work effectively in international teams.
Participants will work on joint projects that require cooperation and exchange of ideas, fostering teamwork and mutual
respect.
Fourth, we aim to equip young participants with key competences and life skills, such as critical thinking, communication,
leadership, and digital literacy, which are essential for their personal and professional development. Developing these skills
prepares young people for the challenges of the modern world, making them more adaptable, employable, and capable of
contributing positively to society. We will offer a variety of skill-building workshops focused on developing specific skills,
such as leadership training, digital literacy courses, and critical thinking exercises. Participants will have opportunities to
apply their skills in real-world contexts, such as community service projects, internships, and volunteering. We will also
promote peer-to-peer learning, where participants teach and learn from each other, fostering a collaborative and supportive
learning environment.
Finally, we aim to motivate young people to take an active role in their communities and promote social responsibility,
encouraging them to contribute to societal well-being and sustainable development. Active participation and social
responsibility are cornerstones of a healthy democracy. Engaging youth in community service and social initiatives raise a
sense of purpose and responsibility. To this end, we will facilitate community-based projects where participants identify local
issues and work together to address them, promoting social responsibility and civic engagement. We will provide
information and opportunities for participants to volunteer in various social and environmental causes. Regular reflection
sessions will be organized where participants can discuss their experiences, challenges, and learnings, reinforcing the
importance of social responsibility.
What activities do you plan to implement? What is the number and profile of the participants involved?
The participants will be young people, in total 32 (plus 4 team leaders) from 4 different countries, aged 18-30 years old.
Participants will come from diverse backgrounds, including students, recent graduates, and young professionals from
various fields. They will have varying levels of study and work experience, bringing a rich mix of perspectives and skills to
the project.

Call 2025 - Round 3
Mobility of young people (KA152-YOU)
Form ID: KA152-YOU-BBD01623 Deadline (Brussels Time): 01 Oct 2025 12:00:00
Common fears among participants may include the fear of public speaking, anxiety about traveling and engaging in new
cultural settings, and concerns about whether their contributions will be valued. Some may also worry about balancing the
project with their other commitments. Participants need a supportive and inclusive environment where they can safely
express their views and ideas. They desire opportunities for personal growth, skill development, and meaningful
engagement with peers from different cultural backgrounds. They also seek recognition for their efforts and a sense of
achievement. Participants will be motivated by the opportunity to enhance their advocacy skills, learn about EU identity, and
engage in intercultural collaboration. They will find happiness in forming new friendships, gaining new experiences, and
contributing to projects that have a positive impact on their communities. By participating in this project, they will gain
valuable advocacy skills, a deeper understanding of EU values and institutions, improved intercultural communication
abilities, and a range of competences such as leadership, critical thinking, and digital literacy.
Some participants may face fewer opportunities due to socioeconomic or geographical isolation. This project aims to be
inclusive and provide equal opportunities for all participants, ensuring that those who face fewer opportunities are given the
support they need to succeed. Moreover, we will ensure gender balance with approximately half of the participants being
male and half being female.
Concerning the achievement of advocacy skills we will implement several activities. One will be about public speaking and
debate training. They will consist in sessions where participants learn and practice public speaking and debate skills
through interactive exercises. Another one about campaign planning and execution workshops on how to design, plan, and
implement advocacy campaigns, where participants will work in groups to develop their own campaigns on issues they are
passionate about.
For the EU identity and values education, we will promote interactive lectures where participants will be engaged in
presentations on the history, values, and functioning of the European Union. They will take part in discussions sharing their
perspectives on EU identity and its relevance to their lives. Finally, role-playing games that simulate EU decision-making
processes, such as a model European Parliament session.
Cultural nights and city tour will let participants share their cultures, traditions, and personal stories, promoting mutual
understanding and respect, and meanwhile to get in touch with a new culture and new tradition regarding Hungary visiting
Budapest and its interesting monuments and spots. Sessions focused on exploring cultural differences and commonalities
through dialogue and creative activities will be implemented. Group projects that require participants to work together
across cultural boundaries, foster teamwork and innovation.
Thanks to this project participants will develop several skills in different sessions: team building, essential digital skills, such
as social media management, digital advocacy, and online collaboration tools, and critical thinking through several activities
designed to enhance participants' critical thinking and problem-solving abilities.
Regular sessions where participants reflect on their experiences, discuss challenges, and share their learnings will be
implemented during the reflection time activity. With the aim to collect participants’ feedback and knowledge about the main
topics and their experience. In fact, thanks to the Youthpass activity participants will understand the Youthpass process and
document their learning outcomes.
Our approach ensures that participants are engaged in meaningful ways, following Roger Hart’s Ladder of Young People’s
Participation. We aim to avoid the lower rungs (1-3), which represent non-participation. Instead, we will ensure that
participants are actively involved in planning, decision-making, and implementation processes, achieving higher rungs (4-8),
which include assigned but informed roles, consulted and informed participation, adult-initiated, shared decisions with
young people, and fully youth-initiated and directed activities.
What results and impact do you expect your project to have?
Our project is designed to create significant and lasting impacts at multiple levels, including participants, local communities,
partner organizations, and Eu society. By focusing on advocacy and EU identity, we aim to foster a deeper understanding of
European values, enhance key competences, and promote active citizenship.
Participants will gain valuable skills in advocacy, public speaking, leadership, digital literacy, and critical thinking. These
skills will enhance their employability and personal development, preparing them for future challenges in their academic and
professional lives. They will develop a stronger sense of European identity and a deeper understanding of EU values and
institutions. This will foster a sense of belonging and unity among young Europeans, encouraging them to actively
participate in the EU’s democratic processes. Through intercultural workshops and collaborative projects, participants will
improve their intercultural communication and collaboration skills. This will help reduce prejudices and promote mutual
understanding and respect among diverse cultural groups. The project will provide participants with opportunities for
personal reflection and growth. Engaging in community service and advocacy will boost their self-confidence, sense of
responsibility, and motivation to contribute positively to society. Establishing an alumni network will help maintain
connections between participants. This network can facilitate continued collaboration, peer support, and the sharing of

experiences and best practices. Participants will be encouraged to continue their advocacy campaigns beyond the project’s
duration. They will receive ongoing support from partner organizations to ensure these campaigns have a lasting impact.
The community-based projects and volunteer activities will directly benefit local communities. Participants will identify and
address local issues, promoting civic engagement and social responsibility among young people. Local communities will
gain a better understanding of EU values and the importance of active citizenship. This increased awareness can lead to
more community members engaging in democratic processes and supporting social initiatives. The skills and knowledge
gained by participants will have a ripple effect as they continue to engage in advocacy and community service beyond the
project. This can lead to sustainable positive changes in their communities. Follow-up workshops and seminars will be
organized in local communities to disseminate the project’s outcomes and continue promoting EU values and active
citizenship.
Partner organizations will benefit from the capacity-building aspects of the project. They will gain access to new tools,
methodologies, and resources for youth work, enhancing their ability to support young people effectively. Collaborating on
this project will strengthen the networks between partner organizations. These connections can lead to future collaborations,
shared best practices, and increased resource-sharing. Moreover they will enhance their reputation and visibility through
their involvement in the project. Successfully implementing and disseminating the project’s outcomes will demonstrate their
commitment to youth development and EU values.
By fostering a deeper understanding of EU values and identity among young people, the project will contribute to a more
cohesive and united European society. Participants will act as ambassadors for these values, promoting them in their
communities. The project will encourage active citizenship and democratic participation among young people. This
increased engagement can lead to more informed and proactive communities, contributing to the health of democratic
institutions. Intercultural collaboration and mutual understanding promoted by the project will help reduce cultural prejudices
and social divides. This will contribute to greater social cohesion and harmony within and between communities.
The project materials related to advocacy techniques, EU values, intercultural communication, and community engagement
will be shared through partner organizations and on SALTO, as well as on our website.
A series of videos and articles documenting the project’s activities and outcomes will be produced. These materials will be
shared on social media, websites to reach a wide audience. Personal testimonials from participants will highlight their
experiences and the impact of the project on their lives. These testimonials will be powerful tools for inspiring others and
promoting the project’s values.
For the non-material outcomes the mental and emotional growth will be expeirenced by participants. They will develop
greater resilience, self-confidence, and a sense of agency, empowering them to tackle future challenges with confidence.
Participants will acquire key competences as defined by Erasmus+, including communication in foreign languages, social
and civic.

Call 2025 - Round 3
Mobility of young people (KA152-YOU)
Form ID: KA152-YOU-BBD01623 Deadline (Brussels Time): 01 Oct 2025 12:00:00
specifically focusing on the challenges, problems, and issues faced by Generation Z youth (18-25 years old youngsters).
Our specialty is that we achieve this primarily by focusing on international projects and non-formal education. We believe in
the power of non-formal education and in the power of Erasmus+. We understand that Gen Z young people are greatly
helped through these project, especially with starting their careers, finding their path, and developing their soft skills.
Our members are exclusively Generation Z youth who want to contribute to their own community and support other young
people. In the association, both the management and the local programs, as well as the international projects, are carried
out by young people, for young people. We believe this is the most effective way to truly make an impact on the lives of other
young people, engage with them and have a positive influence on our environment.
We can identify with the fact that this generation has completely different needs and is driven by different topics compared
to previous generations.
Accordingly, there are three topics that we prioritize and consider the most important in our organization: the mental health
of Gen Z young people; environment protection and sustainability; and the challenges related to the digital world, particularly
social media.
We also believe in youth empowerment. It is important to mention that our association operates on a similar principle to a
startup: if any of our members have a good idea or a passion project, the Street of Youth Association provides the
opportunity and a supportive environment for them to realize it.
What are the activities and experience of the organisation in youth work? Please provide information on your organisation’s
/ group’s regular youth work activities.
Although we are a young association, established only in February 2023, our members, who are mostly university students
and young professionals, have significant experience as participants in Erasmus+ youth exchanges, training courses, and
youth participation projects. We are working to utilize our experience and knowledge to create high-quality non-formal
learning mobility opportunities.
Street of Youth Association is a newcomer organization in Erasmus+. However, we are currently working hard on projects in
the previously mentioned topics, and we want to tackle the challenges we have experienced with other young people and
make an impact on our environment and young peers through non-formal learning.
Regarding the mental health of Gen Z young people, we particularly focus on work-life balance, the challenges around
starting careers, and excessive online presence. For the latter, a podcast series is in the pipeline, led by young people and
featuring experts, which is currently in the planning phase.
Regarding environmental protection and sustainability, the topics of climate anxiety, sustainable habits, and climate activism
are of particular concern to our organization and members. We are currently preparing an Erasmus+ youth exchange aimed
at developing sustainable habits, which may take place in 2025.
Regarding the challenges arising from digitalization among young people, our focus is on the conscious use of social media,
mindful media consumption and critical thinking, as well as screen time management.
We place special emphasis on organizing as many local programs as possible, or our members and other interested young
people so that members can connect offline, such as hiking together, playing soccer, and board games.
As well as promoting Erasmus+, Visegrad, and other international short-term mobility programs, to provide young people
with broad opportunities to participate in these initiatives. We have noticed that young people have limited access to these
opportunities, and many are not even aware of them. We want to democratize this as much as possible and enable more
young people to explore Europe, connect with their European peers, and learn about topics that concern them through non-
formal opportunities. We pay special attention to social media in this regard.
Please give information on the key staff/persons involved in this application and on the competences and previous
experience that they will bring to the project.

- BSc in International Business at BME, Masters in Finance at ELTE

- Specialised in History, active in fields of cultural and political dialogues as well as human rights
- Active in nature with outdoor activities as well as hikes and trips
- Managing partnerships and ongoing projects at local and international level as well- Many Erasmus+ project experiences
individually, so the management of projects and partnerships are in good and experienced hands
Vice-President – Gergő Fazekas

Background and experience
Please briefly present the organisation / the group
ProAtlântico is a nonprofit youth association, founded in 2001, in Oeiras, and it carries out projects with children, youngsters
and disabled people.
ProAtlântico started to develop activities in a local level like sports tournaments, National and International exchanges,
Summer Camps, Youth Centre, entertaining and cultural activities, animation of school breaks and as educational support
after school for children and young people. Soon, came the challenge to work with seniors and with people with disabilities
in Summer Camps, New Year’s Eve, cultural trips and weekend leisure time activities.
In 2003, ProAtlântico started to work with Youth Programme and since then offered the chance to hundreds of young people
to take part in several youth exchanges, training courses and seminars around Europe and soon after became a coordinator
of youth exchanges in Portugal.
Today, ProAtlântico has more than 12.000 members all over Portugal and gives special attention to needed people and to
those with fewer opportunities, aiming to give them the chance to establish contact with other cultures, in order to help them
to find ways to deal with their own problems and to break the existing prejudices.
What are the activities and experience of the partner organisation in youth work? Please provide information on your
organisation’s / group’s regular youth work activities at local level.
In 2003 we started to work in Youth Programme and since them we took part in more than 150 youth exchanges, we also
took part in several training courses and seminars all around Europe.
In Portugal, almost every year we organize 1 or 2 youth exchanges and in 2015 we organized the training course "Let's Play
Together" under Erasmus + programme.
Since 2006 we organized the following exchanges in Portugal:
- Human Rights are Yours and Mine (2006)
- European Citizenship (2007)
- Sports More than a Lifestyle! A tool to promote active citizenship (2009)
- Stop Poverty Now (2010)

administrative and personal support during the projects. Most of the workers are already certified trainers.The organization's
work is also supported by hundreds of volunteers in local and international projects and also by former EVS volunteers who
assist ProAtlântico team under the Erasmus + program.
Our team participates each year in different trainings in Portugal and abroad in order to develop a better work every day. In
2018, we participated in the following trainings:

Project Rationale
In this section, you are asked to explain the aims of your project, what you want to achieve and what are its expected
benefits on participants and participating organisations. Make sure to read the questions carefully and address all sub-
questions.
Needs and Objectives
Why do you want to carry out this project? Please describe the issues and needs you want to address and your project’s
objectives.
We propose a youth exchange project centered on democracy, identity, and values to address pressing issues faced by
young European people today. This project is designed to provide participants with critical thinking skills, awareness of their
choices, and an understanding of common democratic values shared among EU countries. By engaging our members and
partners in this collaborative effort, we aim to create a transformative experience that empowers young individuals to
actively participate in democratic processes and appreciate the diverse identities within the European Union.
Another significant issue is the limited awareness of democratic values. There is a growing disconnect between young
people and the democratic principles that underpin our societies, which has led to lower participation rates in democratic
processes. For instance, Eurostat data shows that youth voter turnout in the EU elections in June 2024 was just 1% more
than the last European Parliament elections of 2019, a good sign but not entirely positive compared to the number of
European citizens. This highlights the urgent and constant need to increase awareness and appreciation of democratic
values such as freedom, equality, and civic participation among young people.
Furthermore, we are witnessing identity and diversity challenges. The rise of nationalism and xenophobia has created
substantial barriers to appreciating and embracing diversity within the EU. According to a European Commission report,
64% of young people feel that discrimination based on ethnicity or religion is a significant issue in their country. To counter
this, we need to foster a sense of shared identity and mutual respect among youth from different cultural backgrounds.
Additionally, there is insecurity about youths’ place in society and how to let them voice be heard. Many young people feel
disconnected from societal structures and believe their ideas and perspectives are not valued or heard. This lack of
positionality contributes to a sense of disempowerment and apathy towards civic engagement. By providing advocacy skills
is an opportunity to express their ideas, helping them to develop a stronger sense of belonging and responsibility towards
their communities.
Moreover, having a speaker from an NGO or the parliament in a workshop on democracy, values, and identities enriches
the learning experience by providing expert knowledge, real-world perspectives, and inspirational role models. These
speakers can bridge the gap between theory and practice, motivating participants to engage actively in their communities
and the democratic process. They also offer valuable networking opportunities and foster critical thinking, making the
project more impactful and meaningful for all attendees.
Thus, in summary, our project aims to address these critical needs:
- Develop critical thinking skills, equipping young people with the ability to critically analyse information, evaluate arguments,
and make informed decisions.
- Raise awareness of democratic values, educating young people about the importance of democratic principles and
encouraging active participation in democratic processes.
- Promote european identity and diversity, fostering mutual respect and appreciation for cultural diversity within the EU.
- Enhance their voices, empowering young people by ensuring their ideas and perspectives are heard and valued,
promoting active civic engagement. This is the case of the advocacy workshop.
Through targeted workshops, interactive seminars and collaborative projects, we aim to create a transformative experience
that empowers young individuals to actively participate in democratic processes, appreciate the diverse identities within the
European Union, and develop a strong, critical, and informed voice in society.
Street of Youth Association is an NGO that wants to help young Europeans understand themselves better. We believe that
celebrating our cultural differences is important for creating a more diverse yet unified Europe. By celebrating our
differences, we can make young people feel like they belong and respect each other, which is important for our continent.
Our initiatives help young people understand and appreciate their cultural heritage and shared European values. This will
help create a generation that is proud of its diversity and committed to our common ideals.
How does your project link to the objectives of the Erasmus programme and those of Youth Exchanges?
Our project aligns closely with the objectives of the Erasmus Programme and Youth Exchanges by promoting critical
thinking, democratic engagement, and intercultural understanding among young Europeans. The project’s emphasis on
non-formal education, international exchange, and participatory activities such as role playing, culture nights and discussion

The core theme of our project directly addresses the Erasmus goal of promoting active citizenship and democratic

the project underscores the importance of European values such as freedom, equality, and respect for human rights. By

Bringing together young people from different EU countries, the project fosters social inclusion and solidarity. Participants

Activities like workshops on advocacy and democracy help participants to understand and appreciate the democratic
foundations of the EU. These activities are designed to strengthen their European identity and commitment to democratic

where they can exchange ideas and perspectives on democracy and participation. This collaborative approach ensures that
learning is a shared and enriching experience.
Also, organising intercultural nights where participants showcase their heritage will help to celebrate diversity and promote a
sense of unity. These events are a testament to the Erasmus objective of enabling intercultural dialogue and understanding.
At the end of the project will be released a Youthpass as a tool to recognize and document the learning outcomes of
participants. By engaging in workshops, discussions, and collaborative projects, participants will develop key competences
such as critical thinking, communication, and civic engagement. They will understand better what it mean work in a team
and building deep trust among each others, creating positive connections among individuals from different countries.
Youthpass will help them articulate these skills, enhancing their employability and personal development. In fact, the project
leverages non-formal education methods, which are integral to Erasmus. Interactive seminars, debates, and role-playing
exercises create a dynamic learning environment where participants can explore democratic values and identity in an
engaging manner.
Impact
How will your project benefit the young participants involved in the project, during and after the project lifetime?
Our project offers a multitude of benefits to the young participants involved, supporting personal growth and community
engagement both during and after the project. Through workshops and debates, participants will learn to critically analyse
information and construct well-founded arguments, enhancing their ability to evaluate news and political discourse. They will
prepare and present presentations and discussions, improving their public speaking and interpersonal communication skills,
essential for effective advocacy. They will have the opportunity to work in diverse, international groups to learn how to
collaborate effectively, appreciate different perspectives, and resolve conflicts.
The project will bring together young people from various countries, allowing them to build international networks. These
connections can lead to future collaborations and a deeper understanding of global perspectives on democracy and
advocacy, providing the sustainability of the project. Moreover, the interaction with peers from different countries will
enhance language skills, promoting better communication and cultural exchange.
Activities focused on democracy and civic engagement will strengthen participants' understanding of their roles as active
citizens and how to be part of their communities and society. In fact, the project will inspire participants to take initiative in
their communities and explore innovative solutions to social challenges, promoting the entrepreneurship of the youth
participants. Using the Youthpass tool, participants will regularly assess their learning progress, reflect on their experiences,
and document their development in key areas. This structured reflection aids in recognizing personal growth and
competencies gained during the pathway.
After
The benefits of the project extend well beyond its duration, impacting participants' personal and professional lives. They will
use the skills and knowledge acquired in the activities in their daily life.
Participants will use social media platforms to promote the knowledge and insights gained from the project, reaching wider
audiences and advocating for democratic values and youth participation.
The knowledge and skills gained during the project will empower participants to take active roles in their local communities,
engaging in civic activities and promoting democratic values. Equipped with enhanced leadership and initiative skills,
participants will be more likely to spearhead local projects and community initiatives.

learns from each other. There will be 4 teams, with 8 participants and 1 group leader in each.
The selection process for participants will be the responsibility of the partner organizations. We designed the guidelines for

the partner organizations during the selection, as well as they have to select at least 2 participants with fewer opportunities.
The participants are students, recent graduates, young professionals, and people from different fields. The participants
must know English at least on a B1 level to communicate effectively. We will also consider participants' backgrounds to
make sure we have a diverse and dynamic group, both considering backgrounds and languages. Each partner organisation
will nominate participants from their country. This approach benefits the project and everyone involved.
Regarding the details of the selection guidelines, we will require each partner to publish an open call. This approach ensures
that the most motivated participants can be selected for the project – in our view, this is what truly fosters effective dialogue
and teamwork. The open call will include questions such as: Why do you want to take part in the project? How does the
topic relate to you? What do you expect to gain from participating?
In addition to motivation, each partner will be asked to form their team in such a way that it includes at least two young
people with fewer opportunities, as well as an equal balance of male and female participants.
Once the selection process is completed, participants will receive a welcome email and will be added to the project’s
WhatsApp group. Partners will also add them to their respective national group chats. This will allow cooperation and
preparation to begin before the project itself, ensuring that participants do not arrive as complete strangers.
Each group will have a group leader. Group leaders must be at least 18 and will help us with communication with
participants, as well as support us in the reflection process and in the evaluation. Group leaders must have experience in
other Erasmus+ programs and/or experience in youth work and be proficient in English. Group leaders must keep in touch
with the project organisers, as they are vital to the project's success. They must make sure that participants and organisers
can communicate easily. They must also help their group members and solve any problems that come up. We will hold with
them an online pre-meeting before the project and we will have group leaders meeting every night.
The project places special emphasis on young people with fewer opportunities. We will give particular attention to those who
face barriers to participation: from each country we aim to involve two participants with fewer opportunities. We will
specifically focus on two groups: young people facing economic obstacles and those facing geographical obstacles. This will
ensure that they, too, can take part in the project and benefit fully from its outcomes.The task of the partner organizations
will be to involve the 2 participants with fewer opportunities in the team and to prepare them before the project. This is why
we selected partner organizations for the project that carry out visible local youth work and are able to effectively involve
young people with fewer opportunities. We are aware that these young people may not necessarily apply through an open
call with a motivation letter submitted online. However, we are committed to their inclusion, which is why we ask for the
support of the partner organizations in selecting and preparing the participants with fewer opportunities — something that
requires local youth work and direct contact with young people.
We have chosen to focus on these two groups because numerous statistics show that young people living in rural areas, as
well as those with disadvantaged economic backgrounds, tend to be more Eurosceptic and feel more distant from the
European Union. With this project, we would like to demonstrate that European Union is not exclusive to anyonbe but it’s for
every citizen.
In order to ensure that young people with economic and geographical barriers can participate fully, we will introduce a
number of additional measures: if a participant’s travel costs exceed the allocated budget, the inclusion support will be used
to reimburse costs that go beyond the travel limit. If needed, we will purchase flight tickets in advance on behalf of
participants and support them in planning their journeys and more.

What will the participants learn about the chosen topic of the activity? Which learning outcomes or competences (i.e.
knowledge, skills and attitudes/behaviours) are to be acquired/improved by participants in the activity?
Participants will gain an in-depth understanding of democratic principles, processes, and the importance of active
citizenship. They will learn about various advocacy techniques, how to influence policy, and the role of advocacy in
promoting democratic values.
Key Learning Outcomes:
- Understanding the fundamentals of democracy and its significance in society.
- Recognizing the importance of active participation in democratic processes.
- Learning different advocacy strategies and their application.
- Developing skills to effectively communicate and advocate for causes.
- Understanding the role of the European Union in promoting democratic values and unity among member states.
Participants will explore the concept of EU identity, its historical context, and its relevance in today’s society. They will
discuss the benefits and challenges of being part of the EU and the importance of solidarity among EU countries.
Key Learning Outcomes:
- Gaining knowledge about the history and development of the European Union.
- Understanding the cultural and political dimensions of EU identity.
- Appreciating the diversity and common values that bind EU countries together.
- Exploring the role of EU citizenship and the rights and responsibilities it entails.
The programme will help develop multilingual skills of the particpants. By discussing, presenting and debating democratic
principles and EU identity in English, participants will improve their communication skills. Participants will also learn to
communicate with people from different countries. While the focus is on democracy and advocacy, participants will develop
skills in data interpretation and analysis. Participants will learn to assess information and use technology to support their
advocacy.
Participants will learn how to create digital content and use digital tools to get support for their causes. The project will help
participants take responsibility for their own learning. Participants will learn to assess themselves, set goals and think
reflectively. By learning and improving, participants will become better advocates and citizens. Participants will understand
their social and civic responsibilities better. The participants will learn about their rights and duties as citizens, the
importance of civic engagement, and how to help their communities. The project will make people more respectful of each
other and committed to democracy.
The advocacy workshop will facilitate the development of entrepreneurial skills, including creativity, innovation, and risk-
taking. Furthermore, participants will learn to plan and implement advocacy campaigns, manage resources effectively, and
work collaboratively to achieve their goals.
Furthermore, participants will enhance their cultural awareness by engaging with peers from different cultural backgrounds.
The participants will investigate the cultural aspects of EU identity, appreciate the diversity within the EU, and express their
cultural identity through various activities and presentations.

What are the basic elements of the activity? Please describe at the very least the venue(s), non formal learning methods
used, aims of the session etc.
We chose Eger as a city for the youth exchange, where cultures and religions meet and which is less known among
international people but definitely deserves a visit.
The venue will be Hotel Unicornis, is located in the city center of Eger, making it an ideal venue for hosting an Erasmus+
youth exchange program. Thanks to its central location, participants can easily access the city's cultural and historical
landmarks, which can complement the program’s professional and intercultural activities.
One of the hotel's greatest advantages is its air-conditioned conference room, equipped with modern technical facilities, that
can accomodate our project’s 38 people. This provides an excellent setting for workshops, and group activities in a
comfortable and professional environment. The smooth implementation of the program is further supported by the hotel’s
comfortable accommodation, as the modern, well-equipped rooms ensure that participants can relax and recharge.
Meals are also conveniently provided on-site, as the hotel has its own restaurant offering a buffet breakfast and lunch and
dinner, including vegetarian, vegan and gluten free meals. This means that we don't have to worry about the cooking, the
hotel's kitchen will prepare the meals. Additionally, participants can enjoy the wellness area, which offers a great opportunity
for relaxation after the intensive activities of the program. The hotel can be reached from the train station easily by walking.
From Budapest, Eger can be reached easily by train,making it accessible to every participating country.
Non-Formal Learning Methods:
The project uses different ways to learn that are fun and make learning more interesting. The methods encourage active
participation, critical thinking and the practical application of knowledge. This section shows some of the non-formal learning
methods we will use. All the workshops and sessions are designed to give participants hands-on experience and
understanding of democracy, advocacy, and EU identity. Through discussions, role-plays and activities, participants will be
encouraged to work together. At the start of the project and each morning, we will do activities to help participants work
together, lead, and collaborate. Energisers will open the morning session and will activate participants. For instance, in the
workshop on advocacy techniques, participants act out a campaign, developing strategies and presenting their ideas. All of
the activities will consider also a reflection about the environment and promote it through the usage of green and eco friendly
material.
In detail, some of the activities proposed will have some group discussions and debates to help students think critically,
communicate well, and defend their views. The programme will include debates on EU issues, sharing perspectives, and
analysing case studies. The cultural night and tour of Budapest will let participants share cultural practices and traditions.
This will help people understand each other better. The participants will also learn how to use digital tools and social media
to create advocacy campaigns. In this session, they will design and launch a social media campaign on an EU issue. The
aim is to improve digital literacy and social media skills for advocacy and communication. The project is about helping
people to grow, understand themselves better and learn more. Participants will use the Youthpass to assess their learning,
set goals, and share insights with their peers. This approach helps participants learn about democracy, advocacy, and EU
identity while developing essential skills through engaging, interactive learning.
The aims and detailed descriptions of the sessions can be found in the timetable.

Project Design
Preparation, support and follow-up
How will you prepare the participants before the start of the activity (e.g. intercultural, linguistic, risk-prevention etc.) and
how will you support them during and after the activity?
To ensure a successful and impactful experience, we have a comprehensive plan for preparing our participants before the
start of the activity.
Firstly, we and the partner organizations will start with the selection process. We will send clear guidelines to the partner
organizations about the selection guidelines. We will create a detailed application form to gather information about
applicants' interests, backgrounds, language proficiency, and motivations. We want to select a diverse group of young
people with a strong interest in democracy and advocacy, and a willingness to engage in intercultural dialogue. Successful
applicants will receive an infopack containing essential details about the project, including objectives, schedule, travel
information, accommodation, health, safety, and meals. They will be also added to the WhatsApp group of the project, as
well as to the group chats of their national team groups.
A preparatory meeting will be held online by us to the team leaders about the project’s goals, structure, and their roles and
about the most important practical information. To ensure participants are well-prepared, they will be assigned small
preparatory tasks such as researching democratic principles, advocacy issues in their communities, and basic EU identity
concepts. As well as they will discuss the preparation for their intercultural night in this meeting, as well as the travel plans.
Participants will complete online forms detailing any physical or mental health conditions, dietary restrictions, and
emergency contacts. A thorough safety briefing in the infopack will cover emergency procedures, local healthcare facilities,
and contacts for project coordinators.
During the Activity
Each group will have a team leader to support them. We will hold every evening reflection sessions with the help of the
group leaders to assess their learning, their needs and their experiences. Then we will hold the group leader meeting, where
we will discuss together the participants feedbacks and also the changes that might be needed for the next day.
Participants can always address their group leaders and the organizers if they have any questions or problems.
During the activity in Budapest, participants will be divided in intercultural groups and we will make sure every group has a
Hungarian speaking participants that can help with translations.
Meals will cater to all dietary restrictions and preferences. Special needs will be accommodated by the hotel.
During the follow-up, we will maintain the WhatsApp and Facebook groups as alumni groups after the project, where
participants can stay in touch, share their thoughts, memories, as well as articles and videos. Additionally, the organizers
will remain available to participants after the project for any questions or issues they may have, and they can also reach out
to the partner organizations.
By engaging young people at every stage, from preparation through follow-up, we aim to create a stable and supportive
environment that supports learning, growth, and active citizenship. This approach ensures that participants are well-
prepared, supported throughout the project, and empowered to continue their advocacy and democratic engagement long
after the project ends.
What measures will you put in place to ensure the safety and protection of participants?
Consider safety in a physical and mental way, one local person is always with the participants outside of the project venue.
Participants will get the ground rules of the project in the infopack and we will also let them know during the first day of the
project, in a specific session about the project and about practicalities.
One of the first measures to ensure the safety and protection of participants will be the Physical Safety Measures.
To ensure the safety of participants outside the project venue, a local coordinator will always accompany the group. This
individual will be familiar with the area and capable of handling any immediate concerns or emergencies that may arise.
All participants will be required to sign a pledge that outlines key ground rules for the duration of the project. This pledge will
include commitments to:
- No Drugs: A strict no-drug policy to maintain a safe and respectful environment.
- Full Participation: An expectation for participants to engage fully in all scheduled activities and sessions.
- Valid Documents: Participants must have valid travel documents and health insurance before joining the project. Health
insurance can be the European Health Card or a private insurance.
Participants must provide proof of health and travel insurance covering the entire duration of their stay. This ensures they
have access to necessary medical care in case of illness or injury.
We will implement hygiene and safety protocols, including:

For our project, we are committed to incorporating sustainable and environmentally-friendly practices.

description of the measures we will take to minimize our environmental impact and raise awareness among participants
about sustainable practices.
Firstly, we will use reusable materials for workshops and activities, such as whiteboards, chalkboards, and refillable
markers. Any printed materials will be minimized and, where necessary, printed on recycled paper using eco-friendly ink.

use tablets, laptops, and smartphones for note-taking and accessing project materials.

printing. We will make this session on the first day of the project, along with the practical informations.

any disposable items, such as plates, cups, and cutlery. This ensures that even single-use items have a minimal
environmental impact. Moreover, each of the participants will be invited to bring their reusable water bottles.
To reach the venue and the accommodation, and to move around the city of Budapest participants will be obliged to use
public transport. The participants will take the the train from Budapest Keleti station to Eger. After that,the participants will
take a short walk to the accommodation.Therefore, the whole travelling of the participants in Hungary can be made by public
transport. We will provide information on the most sustainable transportation options available.

supports local farmers and reduces the carbon footprint associated with food transportation. To lower the environmental
impact, we will offer vegetarian and vegan meal options. These diets generally require fewer resources and produce less
greenhouse gas emissions. We will discuss with the accommodation zero-waste food policy and that they should make
portions according to the needs of the participants to avoid unnecessary food waste.

behind them. This constant reinforcement helps to keep sustainability at the forefront of participants' minds. If there are any
non-sustainable practices by the participants, the facilitators will warn them.

of our Erasmus Youth Exchange project but also to impose a deep respect and commitment to sustainability among
participants. This approach aligns with our project's broader themes of advocacy and EU identity, ensuring that we
contribute positively to both the personal development of our participants and the health of our environment.

Evaluation
How will you evaluate your project’s success? Which activities will you carry out in order to assess whether, and to what
extent, your project has reached its objectives and results?
Evaluating the success of our project crucial to ensure that we have met our objectives and achieved the desired outcomes.
We will employ a multi-faceted evaluation approach to assess the project's impact comprehensively.
We will develop detailed surveys to be administered at the beginning and the end of the project. The pre-project survey will
assess participants' baseline knowledge, attitudes, and skills related to EU identity, advocacy, and their expectations for the
project. The post-project survey will measure changes in these areas, providing a clear picture of participants' development.
The surveys will include a mix of quantitative and qualitative questions to capture a broad range of data. Questions will
cover:
- Knowledge of EU identity and advocacy.
- Skills development (e.g., communication, teamwork, critical thinking).
- Attitudes towards the EU and active citizenship.
- Satisfaction with the project activities and logistics.
- Personal reflections on growth and learning experiences.
The results of the pre- and post-project surveys will be compared to identify significant changes and measure the project's
impact. This data will be analyzed statistically and qualitatively to provide a comprehensive evaluation report.
Self-Evaluation will happen during the reflection time where participants will engage in daily reflection sessions to self-
evaluate their progress, focusing on the competences outlined in the Youthpass. This will help them document their learning
journey and recognize their development. The individual one where each participant will complete a structured reflection
form daily, with questions designed to prompt deep self-assessment and connection to the day's activities. A calm and
conducive environment will be created for reflection, including soft background music to facilitate focused and thoughtful
reflection.
Importantly, in the last 5-10 mintues of every activity, the participants can reflect on the activity, what they have learned,
what was the aim of the session and how they can use the knowledge they gained.
We will provide also group reflection and feedback where participants will discuss their experiences and insights in smaller
groups, providing a platform for peer learning and mutual support. Each group will document the key points of their
discussion and feedback, which will be shared with the larger project coordinators in order to change and assess the needs
of the participants.
We will collect detailed feedback from our project partners during the follow-up meeting of the project to assess their
satisfaction with the partnership and gather suggestions for improvement.
During the resubmission of the project, we defined new quantitative indicators that help objectively assess the project's
success. These include:
• Gender ratio: Measuring the diversity of the target group, specifically whether equal participation of genders was achieved
in the project.
• Activity within the program: What percentage of participants attended all program days? Naturally, we aim for 100%, but
illness or other unforeseen circumstances may occur.
• Feedback rate: What percentage of participants completed the end-of-project evaluation survey? Here, only 100% is
acceptable.
• Number of created contents: How many posts, videos, images, or other media contents were produced during the project?
• Number of reaches: How many people were reached through social media posts published during the project?
• Number of interactions: How many likes, shares, and comments did the project posts receive?

How will you make your project visible outside your organisation and partner organisations? How will you share its results

Disseminating the results of our project effectively is critical to maximizing its impact and ensuring that the knowledge and
experiences gained reach a broad audience. To achieve this, we have developed a multi-faceted dissemination strategy that
leverages the power of social media, digital platforms, physical events, and participant engagement. Here’s how we will
make our project visible outside our organization and partner organizations.
We will engage social media and participants as the project’s ambassadors. Participants will have a mandatory
dissemination task to share their experiences, learnings, and the project's outcomes on their own social media accounts. By
doing so, they will act as ambassadors for the project, reaching their personal networks and communities. Encouraging
participants to create and share content such as blog posts, photos, videos, and testimonials related to their involvement in
the project will provide authentic insights and help attract a wider audience. We are targeting mainly young people aged 18-
30 with our posts, stories and videos. These posts will provide personal perspectives and help to humanize the project’s
impact.
We will write and publish an article that detail the project's objectives, activities, and outcomes on our website. These
articles will be shared on our website, partner websites too.
It is important for us to involve our partners in the dissemination as well: each of them will also share a post about the project
on their social media channels, for which we will provide them with high-quality photos and videos via our Google Drive. In
this way, their followers – primarily young people aged 18–30 – will also become more familiar with the project’s topic, the
experiences of their peers, and the opportunities offered by Erasmus+ youth exchanges.
At the end of the project we will create a handbook that will be a key resource, detailing the project’s methodologies,

materials Its content will be created collaboratively by participants, ensuring it reflects their insights and experiences. The

wide audience.
